title: ornithocephalus zamoranus
intro: Ornithocephalus zamoranus, also known as Zamora's bird-headed orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid found in the tropical forests of the Philippines. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The extracts from Ornithocephalus zamoranus have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or alcohol to create teas, tonics, and extracts that can be used to treat various health conditions.
